referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: A typed permission mode, rule, result, path validation, and command classifier layer separates what the agent wants to do from what the local environment may allow.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has risk-policy JSON, permission modes, approval records, and GVC risk gates, but rules are still spread across phase verifiers and runner code.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: No single reusable permission rule parser/result object is shared across Local Agent, GVC runner, Codex Context Gateway, and future external tool bridges.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Create a PME permission-rule DSL and shared evaluation result shape before expanding autonomous mutation surfaces.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Shell execution is modeled as a classified command with read-only checks, output limits, shell-specific safety rules, and UI-visible semantics.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME runs explicit verifier commands and blocks deploy/provider/chat operations, but command classification is mostly phase-specific.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: PowerShell and shell safety are not yet centralized as reusable parse/classify/explain primitives for owner-facing automation.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Add a dry-run command classifier that labels read-only, mutation, forbidden, provider-risk, secret-risk, and deploy-risk commands without executing them.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Session state, query context, read/edit context, and restore history are distinct layers instead of one loose transcript.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has project-brain state, Codex Context packs, stale guards, and runner state files.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: PME lacks a compact unified session ledger connecting task intent, files read, edits planned, edits applied, verifier state, and resume pointer.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Unify project-brain, context pack, and execution history into a bounded session ledger with freshness and resume fields.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Approval is a first-class state transition that can be previewed, explained, recorded, and consumed by tool permission checks.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME already uses approval packets, approval preview surfaces, runner control files, and provider approval schemas.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: Approval semantics are strong but fragmented by phase and product area; not all approval records share one canonical schema.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Define an approval-intent envelope shared by Local Agent, GVC runner, Provider gate, and external tool candidates.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Memory files are discoverable, typed, age-aware, and relevance-filtered instead of treated as one global instruction blob.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has AGENTS managed rules and project-brain JSON files.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: Project memory is not yet relevance-ranked or age-scored before each autonomous runner loop.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Add memory age/relevance scoring for project-brain inputs and AGENTS managed state.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Tool bridges benefit from schema caching, output storage, validation, merged command/tool registries, and approval-aware connection setup.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has Codex Context Gateway previews and external tool bridge designs, but not a general local MCP registry for PME tools.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: No shared local tool schema cache or output ledger exists for all owner-facing tools.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Build a read-only PME tool registry preview before any real tool execution bridge.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Diffs are turn-scoped, structured, reviewable, and connect file edit intent to an approval/apply path.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has patch proposal, approval policy, approved patch runner, and autonomous mutation plan/evidence.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: Owner UI does not yet expose one consistent structured diff view for GVC mutations, Local Agent patches, and future tool edits.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Add a unified read-only diff ledger and structured diff panel before adding more write surfaces.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Edits should be paired with operation tracking, visible diffs, rollback manifests, and explicit exit/restore flows.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has rollback manifests, rollback simulation, route disable plans, and GVC rollback on verifier failure.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: Rollback evidence is present, but not yet normalized into one owner-readable undo history across subsystems.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Create a cross-phase rollback ledger with last-known snapshot, verifier failure cause, rollback status, and owner-readable summary.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Terminal interaction is bounded by viewport, output handling, focus state, and shell-specific display components.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has manual launch scripts and runner logs/evidence, but limited terminal output classification.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: Long command output and terminal state are not centrally summarized for owner control panels.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Introduce terminal transcript summaries and output limit policies for all local runner commands.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Agent loops should isolate agent memory, spawn/fork boundaries, progress state, and final result capture.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has GVC next-action selection, timed runner, quality gate, and low-risk mutation executor.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: PME runner is policy-rich but still lacks explicit per-agent memory snapshots and forked subtask result contracts.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Add an autonomous-runner task capsule with memory snapshot, allowed files, mutation plan, result, and rollback pointer.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Config is loaded, validated, migrated, redacted, and explained before affecting runtime behavior.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has control/policy JSON and redacted config preview tools.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: Policy config migration/versioning is not yet uniform for project-brain, runner-control, and approval packets.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Version project-brain/control schemas and add migration checks before the runner reads them.

referenceArchitecturePoints.architecturalPattern: Provider/model selection should be capability-aware, config-aware, and separated from direct execution.
referenceArchitecturePoints.pmeCurrentUse: PME has provider registry, credentialRef gates, model library status rules, and cost/quota/provider execution guards.
referenceArchitecturePoints.gap: Provider abstraction is strong in AI Gateway, but it is intentionally not connected to GVC autonomous runner or Claude Code source audit.
referenceArchitecturePoints.recommendation: Keep Provider bridges approval-gated and reuse only the abstraction pattern, not source code or direct runtime behavior.

pmeUsedPatterns.mechanism: Patch proposal and approved local apply boundary
Context gateway with freshness and relevant-file scope

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: No single reusable permission rule parser/result object is shared across Local Agent, GVC runner, Codex Context Gateway, and future external tool bridges.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Reduces drift between UI approvals, GVC risk gate, and local operation policy.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Create a PME permission-rule DSL and shared evaluation result shape before expanding autonomous mutation surfaces.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: PowerShell and shell safety are not yet centralized as reusable parse/classify/explain primitives for owner-facing automation.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Prevents unsafe command execution and improves owner-visible explanations.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Add a dry-run command classifier that labels read-only, mutation, forbidden, provider-risk, secret-risk, and deploy-risk commands without executing them.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: PME lacks a compact unified session ledger connecting task intent, files read, edits planned, edits applied, verifier state, and resume pointer.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Improves long-running runner resume and auditability.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Unify project-brain, context pack, and execution history into a bounded session ledger with freshness and resume fields.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: Approval semantics are strong but fragmented by phase and product area; not all approval records share one canonical schema.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Reduces fragmented approval packets and dry-run/real-write confusion.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Define an approval-intent envelope shared by Local Agent, GVC runner, Provider gate, and external tool candidates.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: Project memory is not yet relevance-ranked or age-scored before each autonomous runner loop.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Keeps autonomous runs focused on fresh, relevant state.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Add memory age/relevance scoring for project-brain inputs and AGENTS managed state.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: No shared local tool schema cache or output ledger exists for all owner-facing tools.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Creates a safer path to future tool integrations without connecting runtime too early.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Build a read-only PME tool registry preview before any real tool execution bridge.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: Owner UI does not yet expose one consistent structured diff view for GVC mutations, Local Agent patches, and future tool edits.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Makes owner review and rollback easier to trust.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Add a unified read-only diff ledger and structured diff panel before adding more write surfaces.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: Rollback evidence is present, but not yet normalized into one owner-readable undo history across subsystems.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Improves recovery evidence after verifier failures.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Create a cross-phase rollback ledger with last-known snapshot, verifier failure cause, rollback status, and owner-readable summary.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: Long command output and terminal state are not centrally summarized for owner control panels.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Keeps local automation understandable and bounded.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Introduce terminal transcript summaries and output limit policies for all local runner commands.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.copyBoundary: Use architecture ideas only. Do not copy Claude Code source into PME.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.gap: PME runner is policy-rich but still lacks explicit per-agent memory snapshots and forked subtask result contracts.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Makes autonomous tasks easier to isolate, verify, and stop.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Add an autonomous-runner task capsule with memory snapshot, allowed files, mutation plan, result, and rollback pointer.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.expectedValue: Prevents stale or invalid policy files from silently changing runner behavior.
gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Version project-brain/control schemas and add migration checks before the runner reads them.

gapsWorthAbsorbing.safeNextStep: Keep Provider bridges approval-gated and reuse only the abstraction pattern, not source code or direct runtime behavior.

licenseAndCopyRisk.risk: Local source tree is a recovered/non-official Claude Code source tree, not a PME-owned dependency.
licenseAndCopyRisk.impact: Direct copying into PME can create license, copyright, and provenance risk.
licenseAndCopyRisk.policy: Do not copy source, comments, or implementation bodies. Keep only pattern-level audit notes and independent PME implementations.

licenseAndCopyRisk.risk: The local package metadata declares license handling outside a normal permissive open-source grant.
licenseAndCopyRisk.impact: Commercial reuse requires independent legal review before any code reuse.
licenseAndCopyRisk.policy: Treat this phase as static architecture research only.

licenseAndCopyRisk.risk: Some modules relate to auth/session/provider behavior.
licenseAndCopyRisk.impact: Unsafe files could accidentally expose secret handling details if blindly scanned.
licenseAndCopyRisk.policy: The audit skips auth.json, .env, token-named, secret-named, archive, dependency, and oversized bundle files.

nextPhaseRecommendations.goal: Define a shared permission rule/result schema for Local Agent, GVC runner, Codex Context Gateway, and future tool bridges.
nextPhaseRecommendations.boundary: docs/spec/verifier only; no runtime tool execution.

nextPhaseRecommendations.goal: Build a dry-run command classifier for PowerShell/cmd/node/pnpm/git/provider/deploy/secret risks.
nextPhaseRecommendations.boundary: classification only; no command execution beyond verifier tests.

nextPhaseRecommendations.goal: Create a read-only tool schema cache and result ledger preview for owner-facing tool governance.
nextPhaseRecommendations.boundary: read-only preview; no MCP connection or external tool invocation.

nextPhaseRecommendations.goal: Unify project-brain, context pack, next-actions, mutation evidence, and rollback pointers into a bounded session ledger.
nextPhaseRecommendations.boundary: local files only; no Provider, no chat route modification.
